No documented consent process for AI data use
Absence of bias testing in health AI models
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, all health AI systems must be suspended until they pass mandatory bias audits, include human domain experts in development, obtain informed consent, and receive ethical committee approval.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Research AI’s harms to mental health
controls.control_text_full: Member States should establish research on the effects and regulation of potential harms to mental health related to AI systems, such as higher degrees of depression, anxiety, social isolation, developing addiction, trafficking, radicalization and misinformation, among others.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must fund and conduct systematic research into how AI contributes to mental health harms like addiction, isolation, or radicalization, and use findings to inform regulatory safeguards.
evidence_signals: National research grants on AI and mental health outcomes
Published studies linking AI use to anxiety or depression
Public health dashboards tracking AI-related mental health trends
Policy briefs recommending mental health safeguards for AI
Collaborations with mental health NGOs on AI impact studies
evidence_anti_signals: No research funding allocated to AI-mental health harms
Absence of public data on AI-related psychological impacts
Health agencies unaware of AI’s mental health risks
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the government should launch a national research initiative to study AI’s psychological impacts and publish annual reports with policy recommendations.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Develop guidelines for human-robot interactions
controls.control_text_full: Member States should develop guidelines for human-robot interactions and their impact on human-human relationships, based on research and directed at the future development of robots, and with special attention to the mental and physical health of human beings. Particular attention should be given to the use of robots in health care and the care for older persons and persons with disabilities, in education, and robots for use by children, toy robots, chatbots and companion robots for children and adults. Furthermore, assistance of AI technologies should be applied to increase the safety and ergonomic use of robots, including in a human-robot working environment. Special attention should be paid to the possibility of using AI to manipulate and abuse human cognitive biases.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must create safety and ethical guidelines for all human-robot interactions — especially in care, education, and child-facing contexts — to prevent psychological harm and exploitation through cognitive manipulation.
evidence_signals: National guidelines for child-facing robots and chatbots
Safety standards for care robots in elderly facilities
Research on cognitive manipulation risks in companion robots
Ergonomic design certifications for human-robot teams
Public awareness campaigns on anthropomorphism risks
evidence_anti_signals: No guidelines for toy robots or companion AI
Care robots deployed without safety or psychological impact reviews
Marketing of robots as 'emotional companions' without disclaimers
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, regulators must issue binding guidelines for all robots interacting with vulnerable populations, including mandatory psychological risk assessments and bans on manipulative design features.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure human-robot interactions comply with human rights principles
controls.control_text_full: Member States should ensure that human-robot interactions comply with the same values and principles that apply to any other AI systems, including human rights and fundamental freedoms, the promotion of diversity, and the protection of vulnerable people or people in vulnerable situations. Ethical questions related to AI-powered systems for neurotechnologies and brain-computer interfaces should be considered in order to preserve human dignity and autonomy.
controls.intent_summary: All human-robot interactions must uphold human rights, diversity, and dignity — especially for vulnerable groups — and neurotechnologies must be regulated to prevent threats to autonomy and identity.
evidence_signals: Human rights impact assessments for neurotech and care robots
Policies prohibiting AI from overriding user autonomy
Diversity audits of robot design teams
Ethical review boards for brain-computer interface research
Public disclosures on neurotech data usage and consent
evidence_anti_signals: Neurotech systems deployed without autonomy safeguards
Robots designed to override user preferences
No diversity requirements in robot development teams
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, all human-robot interaction systems must be halted until they pass human rights and autonomy compliance reviews, with special scrutiny for neurotechnologies.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure users can identify and refuse AI interactions
controls.control_text_full: Member States should ensure that users can easily identify whether they are interacting with a living being, or with an AI system imitating human or animal characteristics, and can effectively refuse such interaction and request human intervention.
controls.intent_summary: Users must always be clearly informed when they are interacting with AI — not a human — and have the right to opt out or request human assistance without penalty.
evidence_signals: Clear labeling of AI systems as non-human (e.g., 'I am an AI')
Opt-out buttons or commands in chatbots and robots
Training for staff to handle AI interaction requests
Complaint mechanisms for forced AI interactions
Public awareness campaigns on AI identification rights
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems impersonating humans without disclosure
No option to escalate to human support
Marketing that blurs AI/human distinction (e.g., 'your friend AI')
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, all AI systems must be redesigned to include unambiguous identification and a one-click human escalation option, with penalties for non-compliance.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Raise awareness about anthropomorphization of AI
controls.control_text_full: Member States should implement policies to raise awareness about the anthropomorphization of AI technologies and technologies that recognize and mimic human emotions, including in the language used to mention them, and assess the manifestations, ethical implications and possible limitations of such anthropomorphization, in particular in the context of robot-human interaction and especially when children are involved.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must educate the public — especially parents and educators — about the risks of treating AI as human-like, and regulate language and design that falsely implies emotion or consciousness.
evidence_signals: Public education campaigns on AI anthropomorphism
Guidelines for marketers on avoiding human-like language (e.g., 'AI feels')
School curricula addressing AI deception risks
Ethical reviews of AI product naming and branding
Parental advisories for child-facing AI toys
evidence_anti_signals: AI products marketed as 'empathetic' or 'conscious'
No public guidance on anthropomorphism risks
Children’s toys claiming to 'understand feelings'
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, regulators must ban misleading marketing of AI as emotional or sentient, and mandate public awareness campaigns on anthropomorphism risks, especially for children.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Promote research on long-term AI impacts on children and youth
controls.control_text_full: Member States should encourage and promote collaborative research into the effects of long-term interaction of people with AI systems, paying particular attention to the psychological and cognitive impact that these systems can have on children and young people. This should be done using multiple norms, principles, protocols, disciplinary approaches, and assessment of the modification of behaviours and habits, as well as careful evaluation of the downstream cultural and societal impacts. Furthermore, Member States should encourage research on the effect of AI technologies on health system performance and health outcomes.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must fund longitudinal studies on how prolonged AI exposure affects children’s development, behavior, and cognition, and assess broader cultural impacts to inform protective policies.
evidence_signals: Longitudinal studies tracking children’s AI interactions
Interdisciplinary research teams (psychology, education, AI)
Public reports on AI’s impact on attention spans or social skills
School-based AI usage impact assessments
Collaborations with child psychologists on AI design
evidence_anti_signals: No research on long-term child-AI interaction
AI tools in schools deployed without developmental impact studies
Absence of child psychology input in AI product design
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the government must establish a national research program to study long-term AI effects on children, with mandatory ethical and developmental impact assessments for all child-facing AI.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Engage children and youth in AI decision-making
controls.control_text_full: Member States, as well as all stakeholders, should put in place mechanisms to meaningfully engage children and young people in conversations, debates and decision-making with regard to the impact of AI systems on their lives and futures.
controls.intent_summary: Children and youth must be given formal, structured opportunities to contribute to AI policy and design decisions that affect them, ensuring their voices shape the future of AI systems.
evidence_signals: Youth advisory councils on AI policy
School-based AI ethics forums with student participation
Public consultations specifically targeting minors
AI development teams including youth representatives
Policy documents citing youth input
evidence_anti_signals: No youth representation in AI policy bodies
AI decisions made without consulting children or teens
Public consultations exclude under-18 participants
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, governments must create mandatory youth participation mechanisms in all AI policy and design processes affecting children, with documented input and response protocols.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Monitor and evaluate AI ethics policies transparently
controls.control_text_full: Member States should, according to their specific conditions, governing structures and constitutional provisions, credibly and transparently monitor and evaluate policies, programmes and mechanisms related to ethics of AI, using a combination of quantitative and qualitative approaches.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must establish ongoing, transparent monitoring systems to assess whether AI ethics policies are working, using both data and stakeholder feedback to ensure accountability.
evidence_signals: Annual public reports on AI ethics policy effectiveness
Mixed-method evaluation frameworks (surveys + metrics)
Public dashboards tracking AI ethics compliance
Independent audits of AI governance programs
Citizen feedback channels for AI policy evaluation
evidence_anti_signals: No public reporting on AI ethics outcomes
Evaluation limited to internal government reviews
No qualitative feedback collected from affected communities
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the government must publish annual, independent evaluations of its AI ethics policies using both quantitative metrics and qualitative stakeholder input, with findings made publicly accessible.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure broad stakeholder participation in AI monitoring
controls.control_text_full: Processes for monitoring and evaluation should ensure broad participation of all stakeholders, including, but not limited to, vulnerable people or people in vulnerable situations. Social, cultural and gender diversity should be ensured, with a view to improving learning processes and strengthening the connections between findings, decision-making, transparency and accountability for results.
controls.intent_summary: AI ethics monitoring must include diverse voices — especially marginalized and vulnerable groups — to ensure evaluations reflect real-world impacts and avoid systemic bias in assessment.
evidence_signals: Inclusion of disabled, elderly, indigenous, and low-income participants in evaluation panels
Gender-disaggregated data in AI impact assessments
Culturally adapted consultation methods for diverse communities
Public records of stakeholder engagement in monitoring
Partnerships with civil society organizations in evaluation
evidence_anti_signals: Evaluation panels composed only of tech experts or government officials
No representation from vulnerable populations
Surveys conducted only in dominant languages or online
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, all AI ethics monitoring processes must be redesigned to include mandatory representation from vulnerable and marginalized communities, with accessible and culturally appropriate engagement methods.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Develop indicators to assess AI ethics policy effectiveness
controls.control_text_full: In the interests of promoting best policies and practices related to ethics of AI, appropriate tools and indicators should be developed for assessing the effectiveness and efficiency thereof against agreed standards, priorities and targets, including specific targets for persons belonging to disadvantaged, marginalized populations, and vulnerable people or people in vulnerable situations, as well as the impact of AI systems at individual and societal levels. The monitoring and assessment of the impact of AI systems and related AI ethics policies and practices should be carried out continuously in a systematic way proportionate to the relevant risks. This should be based on internationally agreed frameworks and involve evaluations of private and public institutions, providers and programmes, including self-evaluations, as well as tracer studies and the development of sets of indicators. Data collection and processing should be conducted in accordance with international law, national legislation on data protection and data privacy, and the values and principles outlined in this Recommendation.
controls.intent_summary: Governments and institutions must create measurable, risk-based indicators to track AI ethics outcomes — especially for vulnerable groups — using ethical data practices and internationally recognized standards.
evidence_signals: Publicly available AI ethics indicator dashboards
Targets set for reducing disparities in AI access or harm
Tracer studies linking AI deployment to outcomes in marginalized communities
Self-assessment templates for AI developers aligned with UNESCO principles
Data collection protocols compliant with GDPR or equivalent
evidence_anti_signals: No measurable targets for vulnerable populations
Indicators focused only on technical performance, not ethics
Data collection without consent or privacy safeguards
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, institutions must develop and publish a set of measurable, equity-focused AI ethics indicators aligned with international standards, with mandatory reporting for all public and high-risk private AI systems.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Establish monitoring mechanisms such as ethics commissions or observatories
controls.control_text_full: In particular, Member States may wish to consider possible mechanisms for monitoring and evaluation, such as an ethics commission, AI ethics observatory, repository covering human rights-compliant and ethical development of AI systems, or contributions to existing initiatives by addressing adherence to ethical principles across UNESCO’s areas of competence, an experience-sharing mechanism, AI regulatory sandboxes, and an assessment guide for all AI actors to evaluate their adherence to policy recommendations mentioned in this document.
controls.intent_summary: Governments should create dedicated institutional mechanisms — like ethics commissions or observatories — to continuously track AI ethics compliance and share best practices across sectors.
evidence_signals: National AI Ethics Commission with public mandate
Public repository of ethical AI systems and case studies
Regulatory sandboxes for testing ethical AI prototypes
National AI ethics assessment guide for developers
Regular public forums for sharing AI ethics experiences
evidence_anti_signals: No dedicated AI ethics oversight body
No public repository of ethical AI practices
No mechanism for developers to self-assess compliance
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the government must establish at least one institutional mechanism — such as an AI ethics observatory or assessment guide — to enable continuous monitoring and public accountability of AI ethics compliance.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Respect and promote UNESCO AI ethics principles
controls.control_text_full: Member States and all other stakeholders as identified in this Recommendation should respect, promote and protect the ethical values, principles and standards regarding AI that are identified in this Recommendation, and should take all feasible steps to give effect to its policy recommendations.
controls.intent_summary: All governments and AI actors must actively uphold and implement the ethical principles outlined in the UNESCO Recommendation, not just acknowledge them — translating them into concrete actions and policies.
evidence_signals: National AI policies explicitly citing UNESCO principles
Corporate AI charters aligned with UNESCO standards
Public commitments by tech firms to UNESCO ethics
Training programs for public officials on UNESCO AI ethics
Legislation incorporating UNESCO recommendations
evidence_anti_signals: AI policies ignore UNESCO principles entirely
No reference to UNESCO in national AI strategies
Corporate ethics statements contradict UNESCO values
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, all public and private AI actors must revise their policies and systems to explicitly align with and operationalize the ethical principles of the UNESCO Recommendation, with public disclosure of alignment efforts.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Cooperate with national and international stakeholders on AI ethics
controls.control_text_full: Member States should strive to extend and complement their own action in respect of this Recommendation, by cooperating with all relevant national and international governmental and non-governmental organizations, as well as transnational corporations and scientific organizations, whose activities fall within the scope and objectives of this Recommendation. The development of a UNESCO Ethical Impact Assessment methodology and the establishment of national commissions for the ethics of AI can be important instruments for this.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must collaborate across sectors and borders — with NGOs, corporations, and researchers — to implement AI ethics, using tools like ethical impact assessments and national commissions as key instruments.
evidence_signals: Public-private partnerships on AI ethics initiatives
Joint research projects with universities and NGOs
Transnational AI ethics task forces
Adoption of UNESCO’s EIA methodology by national agencies
National AI ethics commissions with multi-stakeholder membership
evidence_anti_signals: AI ethics work done in isolation by government alone
No collaboration with civil society or industry
Absence of national AI ethics commission
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, governments must establish formal collaboration frameworks with NGOs, industry, and academia to co-develop and implement AI ethics policies, including adopting UNESCO’s EIA methodology.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Interpret the Recommendation as a holistic and interrelated framework
controls.control_text_full: This Recommendation needs to be understood as a whole, and the foundational values and principles are to be understood as complementary and interrelated.
controls.intent_summary: The ethical principles in the Recommendation must not be applied in isolation; they are meant to be interpreted together as an integrated framework to avoid selective or contradictory implementation.
evidence_signals: Documentation showing how multiple principles (e.g., fairness, transparency, accountability) are addressed together in AI design
Ethical review boards evaluating AI systems using the full set of principles
Training materials emphasizing interconnectedness of values
Audit reports referencing multiple principles jointly
Policy documents explicitly stating that principles are non-severable
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems evaluated against only one principle (e.g., only transparency)
Separate policies for each principle with no cross-referencing
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should revise its AI governance documents to explicitly require that all ethical principles be applied in an integrated and mutually reinforcing manner.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: No interpretation undermines international law or human rights
controls.control_text_full: Nothing in this Recommendation may be interpreted as replacing, altering or otherwise prejudicing States’ obligations or rights under international law, or as approval for any State, other political, economic or social actor, group or person to engage in any activity or perform any act contrary to human rights, fundamental freedoms, human dignity and concern for the environment and ecosystems, both living and non-living.
controls.intent_summary: The Recommendation cannot be used to weaken existing human rights or environmental obligations under international law; any AI activity violating these rights is explicitly prohibited, regardless of compliance with the Recommendation.
evidence_signals: Legal review confirming AI systems comply with ICCPR, CRC, CEDAW, etc.
AI procurement clauses prohibiting systems that violate human rights
Public statements disavowing AI uses that infringe on dignity or environment
Ethics review boards empowered to block AI projects violating international law
Audit trails showing human rights impact assessments conducted
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems deployed that discriminate or violate privacy without legal justification
Claims that 'UNESCO Recommendation permits' activities violating human rights
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization must immediately halt any AI activity violating human rights or environmental protections and conduct a legal compliance audit against binding international treaties.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: UNESCO to promote and disseminate the Recommendation
controls.control_text_full: UNESCO has the vocation to be the principal United Nations agency to promote and disseminate this Recommendation, and accordingly will work in collaboration with other relevant United Nations entities, while respecting their mandate and avoiding duplication of work.
controls.intent_summary: UNESCO is mandated to lead global awareness and adoption of the Recommendation through coordinated efforts with other UN agencies, ensuring consistent messaging and avoiding overlap.
evidence_signals: UNESCO-hosted global AI ethics webinars or toolkits
Publications or reports co-branded with other UN agencies on AI ethics
Official UN system-wide communication referencing this Recommendation
UNESCO’s website featuring a dedicated AI ethics portal with implementation resources
Reports from UNDP, UNICEF, etc. citing UNESCO’s AI ethics framework
evidence_anti_signals: Other UN agencies promoting conflicting AI ethics standards
Absence of UNESCO’s AI ethics content on official UN portals
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should reference UNESCO’s official dissemination materials and align its own communications with UNESCO’s global advocacy efforts.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: UNESCO to collaborate with international, regional and sub-regional organizations
controls.control_text_full: UNESCO, including its bodies, such as the World Commission on the Ethics of Scientific Knowledge and Technology (COMEST), the International Bioethics Committee (IBC) and the Intergovernmental Bioethics Committee (IGBC), will also work in collaboration with other international, regional and sub-regional governmental and non-governmental organizations.
controls.intent_summary: UNESCO and its internal ethics bodies are required to coordinate with regional and global organizations to ensure consistent application of the Recommendation across different contexts and jurisdictions.
evidence_signals: Joint statements or guidelines issued with regional bodies (e.g., African Union, EU)
Partnerships with regional AI ethics networks documented on UNESCO’s website
Training programs co-delivered with regional NGOs or academic consortia
Reports from COMEST/IBC referencing collaboration with regional entities
Funding or technical support provided to regional AI ethics initiatives
evidence_anti_signals: No evidence of collaboration with regional organizations on AI ethics
Regional AI policies developed without reference to UNESCO’s framework
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should initiate or document at least one formal collaboration with a regional or international body to align AI ethics practices with UNESCO’s framework.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Ensure civil society’s legitimacy in advocating for public interest
controls.control_text_full: Even though, within UNESCO, the mandate to promote and protect falls within the authority of governments and intergovernmental bodies, civil society will be an important actor to advocate for the public sector’s interests and therefore UNESCO needs to ensure and promote its legitimacy.
controls.intent_summary: UNESCO must actively support and legitimize civil society’s role in holding governments and corporations accountable for ethical AI practices, recognizing civil society as a key guardian of public interest.
evidence_signals: Public consultations with civil society organizations on AI policy drafts
Funding or platforms provided to NGOs for AI ethics advocacy
Civil society representatives included in UNESCO’s AI ethics advisory groups
Reports authored by civil society cited in official UNESCO publications
Mechanisms for civil society to report AI ethics violations to UNESCO
evidence_anti_signals: Exclusion of civil society from AI ethics consultations
Government policies developed without civil society input
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should establish a formal channel for civil society input into AI governance decisions and publicly acknowledge their role in upholding ethical standards.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Application des principes éthiques de l’IA conformément au droit international des droits de l’homme
controls.control_text_full: Recommande aux États membres d’appliquer, à titre volontaire, les dispositions de la présente Recommandation en prenant des mesures appropriées, notamment législatives, conformes aux pratiques constitutionnelles et aux structures de gouvernance de chaque État, en vue de donner effet, dans leurs juridictions, aux principes et normes énoncés dans la Recommandation conformément au droit international, y compris le droit international des droits de l’homme.
controls.intent_summary: States must implement the ethical principles of the UNESCO AI Recommendation through appropriate legal and governance measures, aligned with their constitutional frameworks and international human rights obligations. This ensures AI systems respect human dignity, rights, and freedoms as foundational norms.
evidence_signals: National legislation referencing the UNESCO AI Recommendation
Constitutional or legal review processes that assess AI systems against human rights standards
Public reports showing alignment of national AI strategies with international human rights law
Government statements committing to implement the Recommendation in line with treaty obligations
Training materials for public officials on human rights-compliant AI governance
evidence_anti_signals: Absence of any legal or policy reference to the UNESCO Recommendation
AI procurement policies that ignore human rights impact assessments
Public statements dismissing international human rights norms as irrelevant to AI
Lack of mechanisms to hold public or private actors accountable for AI-related rights violations
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should immediately integrate the UNESCO AI Recommendation’s ethical principles into its legal and policy frameworks, ensuring alignment with international human rights standards through binding governance mechanisms.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Engagement of all stakeholders in the implementation of AI ethics
controls.control_text_full: Recommande également aux États membres d’impliquer toutes les parties prenantes, y compris les entreprises, afin de veiller à ce qu’elles jouent leurs rôles respectifs dans la mise en œuvre de la Recommandation, et de porter la présente Recommandation à la connaissance des autorités, organismes, instituts de recherche et organismes universitaires, et institutions et organisations des secteurs public, privé et de la société civile engagés dans les technologies de l’IA, afin que le développement et l’utilisation des technologies de l’IA soient guidés par des recherches scientifiques de qualité ainsi que par une analyse et une évaluation éthiques.
controls.intent_summary: All stakeholders—including governments, private companies, researchers, civil society, and academia—must be actively engaged in implementing AI ethics, ensuring that development and use of AI systems are guided by rigorous scientific research and ethical evaluation.
evidence_signals: Stakeholder consultation records with industry, civil society, and academia
Publicly available ethics review boards involving diverse actors
Funding mechanisms requiring multi-stakeholder participation in AI projects
Publicly disclosed AI governance charters signed by multiple stakeholder groups
Training programs for developers that include input from human rights organizations
evidence_anti_signals: AI development led exclusively by private corporations without external oversight
Absence of civil society or academic representation in AI ethics committees
No public documentation of stakeholder engagement processes
Ethics reviews conducted solely by internal teams with no external accountability
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization should establish a formal multi-stakeholder governance body that includes representatives from civil society, academia, industry, and government to oversee AI development and ensure ethical evaluation is integrated at every stage.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Ethical assessment of AI across its entire lifecycle
controls.control_text_full: les questions éthiques concernant les systèmes d’IA se rapportent à toutes les étapes du cycle de vie de ces systèmes, compris ici comme allant de la recherche, la conception et le développement au déploiement et à l’utilisation, et incluant la maintenance, l’exploitation, la commercialisation, le financement, le suivi et l’évaluation, la validation, la fin de l’utilisation, le démontage et la mise hors service.
controls.intent_summary: Ethical considerations must be applied to every phase of an AI system’s lifecycle—from research and design through deployment, maintenance, and decommissioning—to ensure ongoing accountability and harm prevention.
evidence_signals: Documented ethics review checkpoints at each lifecycle stage
Audit trails showing ethical assessments during development, deployment, and decommissioning
End-of-life protocols for AI systems addressing data deletion and environmental impact
Funding or procurement criteria requiring lifecycle ethics assessments
Public reports on AI system performance and ethical risks over time
evidence_anti_signals: Ethics reviews limited to initial design phase only
No documentation of ethical considerations during maintenance or decommissioning
AI systems deployed without plans for monitoring or retirement
Commercial contracts that exclude ethical obligations after sale
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization must implement a mandatory ethics review process at every stage of the AI lifecycle, from conception to decommissioning, and document all assessments for transparency and accountability.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Addressing algorithmic bias and discrimination
controls.control_text_full: De nouveaux défis éthiques sont générés par la reproduction et le renforcement potentiels des biais existants par les algorithmes d’IA, ce qui exacerbe des formes déjà existantes de discriminations, de préjugés et de stéréotypes.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be designed and monitored to prevent the amplification of existing societal biases, discrimination, and stereotypes, ensuring fairness and non-discrimination in automated decision-making.
evidence_signals: Bias audits conducted on training data and model outputs
Diverse datasets used to mitigate representation gaps
Public documentation of fairness metrics and mitigation strategies
Independent third-party evaluations of algorithmic discrimination
Training programs for developers on identifying and reducing bias
evidence_anti_signals: Use of homogeneous or unrepresentative training data
No testing for disparate impact across demographic groups
Absence of documentation on bias mitigation efforts
Claims of 'neutral' algorithms without empirical validation
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization must conduct comprehensive bias audits across all AI systems, implement mitigation strategies based on diverse data and inclusive design, and publicly report on fairness outcomes.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Promotion of global equity and inclusion in AI development
controls.control_text_full: Soulignant qu’il est nécessaire de prêter une attention particulière aux pays à revenu faible et intermédiaire, y compris, sans s’y limiter, aux pays les moins avancés (PMA), aux pays en développement sans littoral (PDSL) et aux petits États insulaires en développement (PEID), car ils disposent de capacités mais sont sous-représentés dans le débat sur l’éthique de l’IA, ce qui soulève des préoccupations quant à une prise en compte insuffisante des savoirs locaux, du pluralisme culturel, des systèmes de valeurs et des exigences d’équité mondiale dans la gestion des incidences positives et négatives des technologies de l’IA.
controls.intent_summary: AI development must actively include and respect the knowledge systems, cultural values, and equity needs of low- and middle-income countries and marginalized regions to prevent global asymmetries and ensure inclusive benefits.
evidence_signals: Partnerships with researchers and institutions from low-income countries
Local language and cultural adaptation of AI systems
Funding programs prioritizing AI projects from underrepresented regions
Inclusion of Global South perspectives in international AI ethics guidelines
Publicly reported data on geographic diversity of AI development teams
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems developed without input from Global South stakeholders
Training data or interfaces that exclude non-Western languages or contexts
Funding or procurement policies that favor high-income country institutions
Absence of any mention of equity or inclusion in AI strategy documents
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization must actively partner with institutions in low- and middle-income countries, incorporate local knowledge into AI design, and ensure equitable access to AI benefits through inclusive development practices.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Respect for cultural diversity and local ethical norms
controls.control_text_full: Tenant pleinement compte du fait que le développement rapide des technologies de l’IA rend difficiles une mise en œuvre et une gouvernance éthiques, ainsi que le respect et la protection de la diversité culturelle, et peut perturber les normes et valeurs éthiques locales et régionales.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must respect and protect local and regional cultural norms and ethical values, avoiding imposition of external standards that may undermine community-specific moral frameworks.
evidence_signals: Cultural impact assessments conducted before AI deployment in specific communities
Local advisory boards providing input on AI alignment with cultural values
AI systems adapted to respect indigenous knowledge systems or religious norms
Public consultations with local communities on AI applications
Documentation of how AI design decisions accommodate cultural context
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems deployed without any cultural context analysis
Standardized AI tools used uniformly across culturally diverse regions
Dismissal of local ethical concerns as 'non-technical'
No mechanisms for community feedback on AI impacts
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, the organization must conduct cultural context assessments for all AI deployments and establish community advisory mechanisms to ensure AI systems respect local ethical and cultural norms.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Environmental sustainability
controls.control_text_full: la présente Recommandation vise à fournir un instrument normatif accepté au niveau mondial qui se concentre non seulement sur la définition des valeurs et des principes, mais aussi sur leur application pratique, par le biais de recommandations stratégiques concrètes, mettant fortement l’accent sur l’inclusion, sur les questions d’égalité des genres et sur la protection de l’environnement et des écosystèmes.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be developed and deployed in ways that minimize environmental harm, reduce energy consumption, and avoid contributing to ecological degradation, aligning with global sustainability goals.
evidence_signals: Carbon footprint estimates for AI training and inference operations
Use of energy-efficient hardware or renewable energy sources for AI infrastructure
Documentation of environmental impact assessments for large-scale AI projects
Policies requiring sustainability criteria in AI procurement
Public reporting on AI-related energy use and emissions
evidence_anti_signals: No tracking or disclosure of energy consumption for AI models
Use of massive, unoptimized models with no efficiency justification
Lack of environmental considerations in AI project planning
Deployment of AI systems with known high ecological costs without mitigation
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, measure and publicly report the environmental impact of AI systems, prioritize energy-efficient architectures, and integrate sustainability criteria into procurement and development guidelines.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Accountability and responsibility
controls.control_text_full: La présente Recommandation s’adresse aux États membres, à la fois en tant qu’acteurs de l’IA et en tant qu’autorités responsables de l’élaboration de cadres juridiques et réglementaires tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA, et de la promotion de la responsabilité des entreprises.
controls.intent_summary: All actors in the AI lifecycle — from developers to deployers to regulators — must be clearly accountable for AI outcomes, with defined roles, oversight mechanisms, and avenues for redress when harm occurs.
evidence_signals: Clear assignment of responsibility roles (e.g., AI owner, auditor, operator)
Legal or contractual liability clauses for AI-related harms
Independent oversight bodies or ethics review boards for AI deployment
Publicly accessible complaint and redress mechanisms for affected individuals
Audit reports with findings and corrective actions tracked to closure
evidence_anti_signals: Vague or absent accountability structures in AI governance documents
Blame-shifting between developers, vendors, and users without clear ownership
No formal process for reporting or resolving AI-related harms
Absence of legal or regulatory consequences for non-compliance
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, define and document clear accountability roles for all AI stakeholders, establish independent oversight mechanisms, and create accessible redress pathways for affected individuals.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Cultural diversity and pluralism
controls.control_text_full: l’identité et la diversité culturelles, car les technologies de l’IA peuvent enrichir les industries culturelles et créatives, mais aussi aboutir à une concentration accrue de l’offre, des données, des marchés et des revenus de la culture entre les mains d’un petit nombre d’acteurs, avec des répercussions potentiellement négatives sur la diversité et le pluralisme des langues, des médias, des expressions culturelles, la participation et l’égalité.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must not homogenize or erode cultural diversity; they must support pluralism in languages, media, and cultural expression, and avoid concentrating cultural power in the hands of a few actors.
evidence_signals: Support for low-resource languages in NLP models and interfaces
Cultural representation audits of training data and content moderation systems
Policies promoting local content creation and distribution via AI tools
Collaboration with indigenous and local cultural institutions in AI design
Transparency about algorithmic curation of cultural content
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems that only support dominant languages or cultural narratives
Algorithmic curation that suppresses minority cultural expressions
Concentration of cultural data and AI tools in a few multinational corporations
Lack of consultation with cultural stakeholders during AI development
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, ensure AI systems support linguistic and cultural diversity, audit for cultural bias in content systems, and partner with local cultural institutions to co-design AI tools.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Human oversight and control
controls.control_text_full: Certaines de ces questions ont trait à la capacité des systèmes d’IA d’effectuer des tâches qu’auparavant seuls des êtres vivants pouvaient réaliser, parfois même uniquement des êtres humains. Les systèmes d’IA pourraient, à long terme, disputer aux êtres humains le sentiment d’expérience et la capacité d’agir qui leur sont propres, ce qui susciterait de nouvelles inquiétudes quant à la compréhension qu’ont les êtres humains d’eux-mêmes, leurs interactions sociales, culturelles et environnementales, leur autonomie, leur capacité d’agir, leur valeur et leur dignité, entre autres.
controls.intent_summary: Humans must retain meaningful oversight and final decision-making authority over AI systems, especially in high-stakes contexts, to preserve human autonomy, agency, and moral responsibility.
evidence_signals: Human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop mechanisms documented in system design
Clear thresholds for when human intervention is required
Training for operators on when and how to override AI decisions
Prohibitions on fully autonomous systems in critical domains (e.g., healthcare, justice)
User interfaces that clearly indicate AI-generated content or recommendations
evidence_anti_signals: Fully autonomous systems making irreversible decisions without human review
Lack of clear human override capabilities in operational systems
Claims of 'self-learning' AI systems that operate without human input
Deployment of AI in life-critical domains without human oversight protocols
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, implement mandatory human oversight protocols for all high-risk AI applications, ensure clear human override mechanisms, and prohibit fully autonomous decision-making in domains affecting human rights or safety.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Equity and non-discrimination
controls.control_text_full: Les acteurs de l’IA doivent promouvoir la justice sociale, garantir l’équité et lutter contre les discriminations en tous genres, conformément au droit international. Cela suppose d’adopter une approche inclusive pour s’assurer que les bénéfices des technologies de l’IA sont disponibles et accessibles à tous, en prenant en considération les besoins spécifiques des différents groupes d’âge, des systèmes culturels, des différents groupes linguistiques, des personnes handicapées, des filles et des femmes, ainsi que des personnes défavorisées, marginalisées et vulnérables ou en situation de vulnérabilité. Les États membres devraient s’efforcer de promouvoir un accès inclusif pour tous, y compris pour les communautés locales, à des systèmes d’IA offrant des contenus et des services adaptés localement, et respectant le multilinguisme et la diversité culturelle. Les États membres devraient s’efforcer de réduire les fractures numériques et de garantir un accès et une participation inclusifs au développement de l’IA. Au niveau national, les États membres devraient promouvoir l’équité entre les zones rurales et urbaines, et entre toutes les personnes indépendamment de leur race, de leur couleur, de leur ascendance, de leur genre, de leur âge, de leur langue, de leur religion, de leurs opinions politiques, de leur origine nationale, ethnique ou sociale, de leur condition économique ou sociale de naissance, de leur handicap ou de tout autre motif en termes d’accès et de participation au cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA. Au niveau international, les pays les plus avancés sur le plan technologique ont un devoir de solidarité envers les pays les moins avancés afin de garantir le partage des bienfaits des technologies de l’IA de telle sorte que l’accès et la participation de ces derniers au cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA contribuent à un ordre mondial plus équitable en matière d’information, de c
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must ensure equitable access and participation for all, especially marginalized and vulnerable groups, by addressing digital divides, supporting multilingual and culturally adapted services, and promoting global solidarity to ensure fair distribution of AI benefits.
evidence_signals: Equity impact assessments covering gender, disability, geography, and language
Localized AI content and services in multiple languages
Programs to support AI access in rural and low-resource communities
International partnerships for technology transfer to developing nations
Public data on disparities in AI access and participation
evidence_anti_signals: AI services available only in dominant languages or urban centers
No efforts to bridge digital divides between regions or populations
Absence of international equity commitments in AI procurement
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, implement targeted equity initiatives to ensure inclusive access, localized adaptation, and international technology sharing, with measurable targets for reducing disparities.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Éviter les applications et résultats discriminatoires ou biaisés
controls.control_text_full: Les acteurs de l’IA doivent faire tout ce qui est raisonnablement possible pour réduire au maximum et éviter de renforcer ou de perpétuer des applications et des résultats discriminatoires ou biaisés, tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA, afin de garantir l’équité de ces systèmes. Un recours efficace devrait être disponible contre la discrimination et la détermination algorithmique biaisée.
controls.intent_summary: AI actors must actively prevent bias and discrimination in AI systems throughout their lifecycle and ensure effective remedies exist for individuals harmed by biased algorithmic decisions.
evidence_signals: Bias audits conducted during development and deployment
Documentation of fairness metrics and thresholds
Publicly accessible grievance mechanism for algorithmic discrimination
Training datasets with documented demographic representation
Remediation logs for flagged biased outcomes
evidence_anti_signals: No bias testing performed
Lack of recourse channels for affected individuals
Use of proxies for protected attributes in training data
No documentation of fairness interventions
controls.remediation_template: Implement and document a bias mitigation strategy across the AI lifecycle, including regular fairness testing, and establish a transparent, accessible process for individuals to challenge discriminatory outcomes.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Protéger la vie privée et les données personnelles
controls.control_text_full: La vie privée, qui constitue un droit essentiel pour la protection de la dignité, de l’autonomie et de la capacité d’action des êtres humains, doit être respectée, protégée et promue tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA. Il est important que les données pour les systèmes d’IA soient collectées, utilisées, partagées, archivées et supprimées d’une manière qui soit conforme au droit international et en accord avec les valeurs et principes énoncés dans la présente Recommandation, tout en respectant les cadres juridiques nationaux, régionaux et internationaux pertinents.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must handle personal data in compliance with international and national privacy laws, ensuring data is collected, used, stored, and deleted ethically and lawfully throughout its lifecycle.
evidence_signals: Data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) for AI systems
Explicit consent mechanisms for data collection
Data retention and deletion policies aligned with GDPR or equivalent
Encryption and anonymization protocols documented
Audit trails of data access and usage
evidence_anti_signals: No documented data handling policy
Use of personal data without consent or legal basis
Lack of data minimization practices
No process for data subject requests (e.g., deletion)
controls.remediation_template: Establish and enforce a data protection framework for AI systems that complies with international standards, including lawful basis for processing, consent mechanisms, and secure data handling practices.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Mettre en place des cadres de protection des données multipartites
controls.control_text_full: Des cadres de protection des données et des mécanismes de gouvernance adéquats doivent être mis en place selon une approche multipartite au niveau national ou international, protégés par les systèmes judiciaires et appliqués tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA. Les cadres de protection des données et tout mécanisme connexe devraient s’inspirer des principes et des normes internationaux de protection de données en ce qui concerne la collecte, l’utilisation et la divulgation des données personnelles et l’exercice de leurs droits par les personnes concernées, tout en assurant ainsi un objectif légitime et une base juridique valable pour le traitement des données personnelles, y compris le consentement éclairé.
controls.intent_summary: Governments and stakeholders must establish multi-stakeholder data protection frameworks grounded in international norms, ensuring legal basis, legitimacy, and enforceable rights for individuals regarding their data in AI systems.
evidence_signals: Multi-stakeholder data governance council with civil society representation
Legal framework referencing GDPR, Convention 108, or equivalent
Publicly accessible data subject rights portal
Judicial oversight mechanisms for data misuse complaints
Training programs for public officials on data protection law
evidence_anti_signals: Single-entity control over data governance
Absence of legal basis for data processing
No mechanism for individuals to exercise data rights
No judicial or regulatory enforcement capacity
controls.remediation_template: Establish a multi-stakeholder data protection governance body with legal authority, aligned with international standards, and ensure individuals can effectively exercise their data rights through accessible and enforceable mechanisms.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Effectuer des évaluations d’impact sur la vie privée
controls.control_text_full: Les systèmes algorithmiques nécessitent des évaluations adéquates de l’impact sur la vie privée, qui incluent également des considérations sociétales et éthiques de leur utilisation et une approche innovante consistant à appliquer le respect de la vie privée dès la conception des systèmes. Les acteurs de l’IA doivent assumer la responsabilité de la conception et de la mise en œuvre des systèmes d’IA de manière à garantir que les informations personnelles sont protégées tout au long du cycle de vie du système d’IA.
controls.intent_summary: AI developers must conduct privacy impact assessments that include societal and ethical considerations, and embed privacy-by-design principles from the earliest stages of system development.
evidence_signals: Privacy-by-design documentation in system architecture
Privacy impact assessments (PIAs) signed off before deployment
Ethical review board approvals for data-intensive AI systems
Design choices documented to minimize data collection
Regular audits of data minimization and anonymization practices
evidence_anti_signals: No privacy impact assessments conducted
Data collected without necessity or proportionality
Privacy considerations added only after deployment
No ethical review process for AI systems
controls.remediation_template: Integrate privacy impact assessments and privacy-by-design principles into all AI development workflows, with documented approvals and regular audits to ensure personal data protection throughout the system lifecycle.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Assurer la surveillance humaine et la responsabilité juridique
controls.control_text_full: Les États membres devraient veiller à ce qu’il soit toujours possible d’attribuer la responsabilité éthique et juridique de tout stade du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA à des personnes physiques ou des entités juridiques existantes, y compris dans les cas de recours relatifs à des systèmes d’IA. Ainsi, la surveillance humaine renvoie non seulement à la surveillance humaine individuelle, mais aussi à une surveillance publique inclusive, selon le cas. Les êtres humains choisiront peut-être, dans certains cas, de s’appuyer sur les systèmes d’IA à des fins d’efficacité, mais la décision de céder les fonctions de contrôle dans des contextes limités reste leur prérogative. En effet, les êtres humains peuvent recourir aux systèmes d’IA pour la prise de décisions et l’exécution de tâches, mais un système d’IA ne peut jamais se substituer à la responsabilité ultime des êtres humains et à leur obligation de rendre compte. De manière générale, les décisions de vie et de mort ne devraient pas être abandonnées à des systèmes d’IA.
controls.intent_summary: Human oversight must be maintained at all stages of AI systems, with clear accountability assigned to human actors; AI must never make life-or-death decisions autonomously, and humans must retain ultimate responsibility and the right to override AI decisions.
evidence_signals: Human-in-the-loop protocols documented for high-risk decisions
Clear assignment of legal responsibility for AI outcomes
Prohibitions on autonomous lethal decision-making in policy
Audit logs showing human review of AI outputs
Training for operators on override procedures and accountability
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems making final decisions without human review
No documented accountability chain for AI actions
Use of AI in life-or-death contexts (e.g., healthcare triage, policing)
Lack of human override capability in critical systems
controls.remediation_template: Ensure all high-risk AI systems have documented human oversight protocols, assign clear legal accountability to human actors, and prohibit autonomous decision-making in life-or-death contexts.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Garantir la transparence et l’explicabilité des systèmes d’IA
controls.control_text_full: La transparence et l’explicabilité des systèmes d’IA sont souvent des conditions préalables essentielles pour garantir le respect, la protection et la promotion des droits humains, des libertés fondamentales et des principes éthiques. La transparence est nécessaire pour que les règlementations nationales et internationales en matière de responsabilité fonctionnent efficacement. Un manque de transparence peut aussi entraver la possibilité de contester efficacement les décisions fondées sur des résultats produits par les systèmes d’IA et porter ainsi atteinte au droit à un jugement équitable et à un recours effectif, et limite les domaines dans lesquels de tels systèmes peuvent être utilisés légalement. S’il convient de tout mettre en œuvre pour améliorer la transparence et l’explicabilité des systèmes d’IA – y compris ceux qui ont une portée extraterritoriale tout au long de leur cycle de vie – afin de favoriser une gouvernance démocratique, le degré de transparence et d’explicabilité devrait toujours être adapté au contexte et à la portée, car il peut être nécessaire de trouver un équilibre entre la transparence et l’explicabilité et d’autres principes, tels que la protection de la vie privée, la sûreté et la sécurité. Les individus devraient être pleinement informés lorsqu’une décision est fondée sur des algorithmes d’IA ou prise par ceux-ci, notamment lorsqu’elle affecte leur sécurité ou leurs droits humains. Dans ces circonstances, ils devraient avoir la possibilité d’exiger ou de demander des explications à l’acteur de l’IA ou aux institutions du secteur public concernés. En outre, les individus doivent pouvoir connaître les raisons qui ont conduit à une décision se répercutant sur leurs droits et leurs libertés, et avoir la possibilité de soumettre des observations à un membre du personnel de l’entreprise du secteur privé ou de l’institution du secteur public habilité à réexaminer et corriger la décision. Les acteurs de l’IA devraient informer les utilisateurs lorsqu’un produit ou un service est fourni directement ou par le biais de systèmes d’intelligence artificielle de manière appropriée et en temps utile. L’explicabilité implique de rendre les résultats des systèmes d’IA intelligibles et de fournir des renseignements à leur sujet. L’explicabilité des systèmes d’IA renvoie également à l’intelligibilité des intrants, des extrants, du fonctionnement des différents modules algorithmiques et de leur contribution aux résultats des systèmes. L’explicabilité est donc étroitement liée à la transparence, puisqu’il convient de rendre les résultats et les sous-processus qui y conduisent intelligibles et traçables, en fonction du contexte. Les acteurs de l’IA devraient s’engager à assurer l’explicabilité des algorithmes mis au point. Dans le cas d’applications de l’IA dont l’impact sur l’utilisateur final n’est pas temporaire, facilement réversible ou à faible risque, il convient de s’assurer qu’une explication valable est fournie avec toute décision ayant entraîné l’action entreprise, afin que les résultats soient considérés comme transparents.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be transparent and explainable, especially when affecting rights or safety; users must be informed when AI decisions impact them and be able to obtain meaningful explanations and challenge those decisions.
evidence_signals: Explanations provided to users for algorithmic decisions (e.g., loan denials, hiring)
Model cards or documentation detailing inputs, outputs, and limitations
User interfaces that disclose AI involvement in decisions
Accessible appeal process for contested AI outcomes
Documentation of algorithmic logic for high-risk applications
evidence_anti_signals: Black-box AI systems with no explanation capability
No disclosure to users that AI is involved in decision-making
No mechanism for users to request or receive explanations
Explanations provided only in technical jargon inaccessible to laypersons
controls.remediation_template: Ensure all high-impact AI systems provide clear, accessible explanations to affected individuals, disclose AI involvement in decisions, and offer a meaningful process to challenge or appeal algorithmic outcomes.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Assumer la responsabilité éthique et juridique pour les droits humains et l’environnement
controls.control_text_full: Les acteurs de l’IA et les États membres devraient respecter, protéger et promouvoir les droits de l’homme et les libertés fondamentales, et devraient également favoriser la protection de l’environnement et des écosystèmes, endossant leur responsabilité éthique et juridique respective, conformément au droit national et international, en particulier les obligations des États membres en matière de droits de l’homme, et aux directives éthiques établies tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA, y compris en ce qui concerne les acteurs de l’IA sur
controls.intent_summary: All AI actors and states must uphold human rights and environmental protection as core obligations throughout the AI lifecycle, aligning actions with international human rights law and ethical guidelines.
evidence_signals: Human rights impact assessments integrated into AI procurement
Environmental sustainability criteria in AI system design
Public commitments to UN Human Rights Council standards
Ethics review boards with human rights and environmental mandates
Reporting on AI’s environmental footprint (e.g., energy use, emissions)
evidence_anti_signals: No human rights or environmental impact assessments conducted
AI systems deployed in violation of international human rights norms
No monitoring of AI’s ecological impact (e.g., carbon emissions)
Absence of ethical guidelines referencing human rights obligations
controls.remediation_template: Integrate human rights and environmental impact assessments into all AI development and deployment processes, and publicly commit to upholding international legal and ethical obligations throughout the AI lifecycle.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Surveillance du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA
controls.control_text_full: Les États membres et les entreprises devraient mettre en œuvre des mesures appropriées pour surveiller toutes les phases du cycle de vie d’un système d’IA dans le cadre de l’évaluation de l’impact éthique, y compris le fonctionnement des algorithmes utilisés pour la prise de décisions, les données, ainsi que les acteurs de l’IA impliqués dans le processus, en particulier dans les services publics et lorsqu’une interaction directe avec l’utilisateur final est nécessaire.
controls.intent_summary: Both states and private actors must continuously monitor all phases of AI systems — including algorithms, data, and human actors — especially in public services and user-facing applications, to ensure ongoing compliance with ethical and human rights standards.
evidence_signals: Continuous monitoring dashboards for AI performance
Data drift and bias detection systems in production
Regular audits of algorithmic behavior in live environments
Documentation of human oversight roles during deployment
Feedback loops from end-users integrated into system updates
evidence_anti_signals: No monitoring after deployment
Monitoring limited to development phase only
No tracking of data quality or model degradation over time
No human oversight during live operations
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, implement a lifecycle monitoring framework with real-time tracking of algorithmic behavior, data integrity, and human oversight, especially for public-facing or high-risk AI systems.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Cadre réglementaire pour les évaluations publiques d’impact
controls.control_text_full: Les gouvernements devraient adopter un cadre réglementaire qui définisse une procédure permettant en particulier aux autorités publiques de mener à bien des évaluations sur l’impact des systèmes d’IA afin d’anticiper les répercussions, d’atténuer les risques, d’éviter les conséquences préjudiciables, de faciliter la participation des citoyens et de faire face aux défis sociétaux. L’évaluation devrait également établir des mécanismes de supervision adaptés, notamment les principes de vérifiabilité, de traçabilité et d’explicabilité, permettant d’évaluer les algorithmes, les données et les processus de conception, et inclure un examen externe des systèmes d’IA. Les évaluations de l’impact éthique devraient être transparentes et ouvertes au public, selon le cas. Elles devraient par ailleurs être multidisciplinaires, multipartites, multiculturelles, pluralistes et inclusives. Les autorités publiques devraient être tenues de surveiller les systèmes d’IA qu’elles mettent en œuvre et/ou déploient, en établissant des mécanismes et des outils appropriés.
controls.intent_summary: Governments must create binding regulatory frameworks requiring public authorities to conduct transparent, inclusive, and externally reviewed ethical impact assessments of AI systems they deploy, ensuring verifiability, traceability, and explicability are embedded in oversight.
evidence_signals: Legally binding regulations mandating AI impact assessments
Publicly accessible assessment reports from government agencies
External review panels for public AI systems
Regulatory requirements for traceability and explicability in procurement
Mandated multidisciplinary teams for assessment design
evidence_anti_signals: No legal requirement for public AI impact assessments
Assessments kept confidential or internal only
No external review or oversight mechanism
Assessments conducted without diverse or marginalized representation
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, enact binding legislation requiring public authorities to conduct transparent, externally reviewed, and inclusive ethical impact assessments for all AI systems they deploy, with enforceable standards for traceability and explicability.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Participation multipartite et inclusion des parties prenantes marginalisées
controls.control_text_full: La participation des différentes parties prenantes tout au long du cycle de vie des systèmes d’IA est nécessaire pour garantir des approches inclusives de la gouvernance de l’IA, permettant que les bénéfices soient partagés par tous, et de contribuer au développement durable. Les parties prenantes comprennent, sans s’y limiter, les gouvernements, les organisations intergouvernementales, la communauté technique, la société civile, les chercheurs et les universitaires, les médias, les responsables de l’éducation, les décideurs, les entreprises du secteur privé, les institutions de défense des droits de l’homme et les organismes de promotion de l’égalité, les organismes de contrôle des mesures de lutte contre la discrimination et les groupes de jeunes et d’enfants. Il conviendrait d’adopter des normes ouvertes et d’assurer l’interopérabilité pour faciliter la collaboration. Des mesures devraient être adoptées pour tenir compte de l’évolution des technologies et de l’émergence de nouveaux groupes de parties prenantes, ainsi que pour permettre une participation effective des groupes, communautés et individus marginalisés et, le cas échéant, dans le cas des peuples autochtones, le respect de leur autonomie dans la gestion de leurs données.
controls.intent_summary: AI governance must involve diverse stakeholders — especially marginalized groups and Indigenous communities — throughout the AI lifecycle, ensuring inclusive participation, open standards, and respect for community autonomy over data.
evidence_signals: Stakeholder engagement plans with defined roles
Inclusion of Indigenous or marginalized groups in design and review
Open standards and interoperable systems documented
Data sovereignty agreements with Indigenous communities
Public consultation records with diverse participants
evidence_anti_signals: Stakeholder engagement limited to industry and government
No representation from marginalized or Indigenous groups
Proprietary systems with no interoperability
No documented consent or autonomy mechanisms for community data
controls.remediation_template: If this control is not met, establish a formal stakeholder engagement process that includes marginalized communities and Indigenous groups, with mechanisms for data autonomy and open standards, documented in governance plans.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Apply proportionality and precaution in AI method selection
controls.control_text_full: Au moment de sélectionner une méthode d’IA, compte tenu de l’importance potentielle des besoins en données et en ressources de certaines d’entre elles et de l’impact sur l’environnement qui en résulte, les États membres devraient veiller à ce que les acteurs de l’IA, suivant le principe de proportionnalité, privilégient les méthodes d’IA économes en données, en énergie et en ressources. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Require AI developers to prioritize low-resource, energy-efficient methods unless higher-impact models are proven necessary and ethically justified.
evidence_signals: Comparison reports selecting efficient models over high-cost alternatives
Documentation justifying use of high-resource AI based on necessity
Policy mandating efficiency thresholds for public AI procurement
evidence_anti_signals: Use of large models without efficiency justification
No evaluation of alternative, lower-impact methods
controls.remediation_template: Adopt procurement and development policies requiring AI developers to demonstrate proportionality and select the least resource-intensive model that achieves the intended outcome, applying the precautionary principle when impacts are uncertain.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure AI promotes gender equality and avoids harm
controls.control_text_full: Les États membres devraient veiller à ce que le potentiel des technologies numériques et de l’intelligence artificielle soit pleinement optimisé afin de contribuer à l’égalité des genres, et à ce qu’il ne soit pas porté atteinte aux droits humains et aux libertés fondamentales des filles et des femmes, ni à leur sécurité et à leur intégrité, à aucun stade du cycle de vie du système d’IA. En outre, les évaluations de l’impact éthique devraient prendre en compte les questions de genre de manière transversale.
controls.intent_summary: Mandate that all AI systems promote gender equality, avoid gender-based harm, and integrate gender analysis throughout their lifecycle via ethical impact assessments.
evidence_signals: Gender impact assessments integrated into AI development pipelines
Audit reports showing reduced gender bias in AI outputs
Policies requiring gender-balanced training data
evidence_anti_signals: AI systems with no gender analysis in testing
Training data excluding female or non-binary representation
controls.remediation_template: Require gender impact assessments for all AI systems, ensure diverse and representative training data, and integrate gender analysis into all stages of AI development and deployment.
controls.framework_name_en: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
controls.framework_name_ar: توصية اليونسكو بشأن أخلاقيات الذكاء الاصطناعي
